1→"""Learn command for extracting insights from plan implementations. 2→ 3→This command discovers all Claude Code sessions associated with a plan 4→and optionally launches Claude with the /erk:learn skill. 5→ 6→Note: Session data retrieval and tracking are handled by separate exec scripts: 7→- `erk exec get-learn-sessions` - Returns JSON with session data 8→- `erk exec track-learn-evaluation` - Posts tracking comment to plan 9→""" 10→ 11→from dataclasses import dataclass 12→from pathlib import Path 13→ 14→import click 15→ 16→from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context 17→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 18→from erk_shared.output.output import user_confirm, user_output 19→from erk_shared.pr_store.types import PrNotFound 20→from erk_shared.sessions.discovery import ( 21→ find_local_sessions_for_project, 22→ get_readable_sessions, 23→) 24→ 25→ 26→@dataclass(frozen=True) 27→class LearnResult: 28→ """Result of learn command.""" 29→ 30→ pr_id: str

Perfect! I have successfully renamed all occurrences in the file. Here's a summary of the changes made to `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/learn/learn_cmd.py`:
## Summary of Changes
All three occurrences of `plan_backend` have been renamed to `pr_backend`:
1. **Line 117**: `ctx.plan_backend.resolve_pr_number_for_branch()` → `ctx.pr_backend.resolve_pr_number_for_branch()`
2. **Line 152**: `ctx.plan_backend.find_sessions_for_managed_pr()` → `ctx.pr_backend.find_sessions_for_managed_pr()`
3. **Line 252**: `ctx.plan_backend.get_metadata_field()` → `ctx.pr_backend.get_metadata_field()`
All changes are attribute accesses on the `ctx` object, and no import statements or function definitions for `require_plan_backend`/`require_pr_backend` were found in this file. The renaming is complete and ready for use.
